instructions: For each source set decision to 'approve' or 'reject'. Note any edits to summary/quote/theme. Then the white paper + keynote regenerate from approved sources and the site rebuilds.
major_changes_to_approve: White paper: add section on the adoption gap (discovery vs. execution) evidenced by CNBC/OpenAI stumble + Forrester mid-2026 reality check — the thesis is real but on a 2-3 year not 12-month horizon.
White paper: add European data lane using McKinsey Europe + Deloitte APAC — the 'decision influence is here, execution is coming' framing is a crisp subheading for the current moment.

White paper: add A2A (agent-to-agent) commerce as the outer-limit scenario using Bain 'Next Retail Revolution' + Mastercard AP4M — where neither human salience nor human trust enters the transaction at all.
White paper: add regulatory section on CMA guidance (March 2026) + IMF macroprudential framework as the first wave of institutional guardrails reshaping the agent-commerce environment.
Keynote: add slide on the 393% AI traffic surge (Adobe Q1 2026) with the conversion-rate reversal (AI traffic now 42% better) as the most compelling single data point for a live audience.

Keynote: update protocol landscape slide to reflect that UCP, ACP, and Mastercard AP4M are all live — protocol fragmentation is real but a consolidation narrative is emerging (most major platforms support UCP/ACP).
Publish white paper v0.1 and keynote v0.1 outline (still marked DRAFT — pending first full approval cycle from Cycle 0).
